title: Sort by Second Element
description: You have a list of tuples: data = [(1, 3), (4, 1), (2, 2)]. Use sorted() with a lambda as the key to sort the tuples by their second element (index 1) in ascending order. Print the sorted list.
starter_code:
data = [(1, 3), (4, 1), (2, 2)]

# Use sorted() with a lambda to sort by the second element
sorted_data = sorted(data, key=lambda x: x[1])
print(sorted_data)
hints: The key parameter of sorted() expects a function that returns the value to sort by
Each tuple x has x[0] as the first element and x[1] as the second element
Your lambda should look like: lambda x: x[1]

code_requirements.description: Uses the lambda keyword for the sort key
code_requirements.fail_message: You must use a lambda function as the key argument to sorted(). Do not define a separate named function with def.
